title: Widget Tree
objectives: Explain the widget tree and its role

sections.markdown: The widget tree is the hierarchy of immutable widget configuration objects you build in build() methods. It's a lightweight description that Flutter diffs against the Element tree to decide what actually changes.

sections.code:
MaterialApp
  └ Scaffold
     ├ AppBar(title: Text)
     └ Center
        └ Column
           ├ Text('Hello')
           └ ElevatedButton

sections.markdown: Three trees work together: the Widget tree (config, rebuilt often & cheaply), the Element tree (the living instances + state), and the RenderObject tree (layout & paint). Flutter reconciles widgets against elements every frame.

Keys preserve identity during reconciliation. Without keys, swapping/reordering items in a list can attach the wrong state to the wrong widget. Use ValueKey/ObjectKey for stateful list items that move.

flashcards.back: The hierarchy of immutable widget config objects produced by build(); cheap to recreate.
They preserve element/state identity when widgets move or reorder during reconciliation.

quiz.explain: Widgets are config; Elements and RenderObjects do the real work.
Keys keep the right state attached when widgets move/reorder.
State objects live with Elements, not the immutable widgets.

challenges.text: Print the widget tree depth for a simple screen.
Reproduce a state-mix-up reordering list items, then fix it with ValueKey.
Use a GlobalKey to access a child's state and explain the cost.
Demonstrate how identical widget types reuse elements while different types rebuild them.

interviewQuestions: What is the widget tree and how does it relate to the other trees?
How does reconciliation decide to reuse vs rebuild?
Why are widgets cheap to recreate?
